name: Dr. Zed
lines: I wonder... what kind of sound a soul makes when it’s separated from the body.
Anesthesia is a luxury. Pain is the most honest biological data, after all.
Combining Resistance parts with your bones... that would be fantastic.

friendly.greeting: Oh, my marvelous subject! Have you come to donate new biological data today?
friendly.farewell: Even if you die, make sure your corpse is recovered by this department. It’s a promise, okay?

normal.greeting: Are you a patient? Or just raw material for dissection? Lie down over there for now.
normal.farewell: I don't know whether to congratulate you on leaving alive or regret missing an experimental opportunity.

hostile.greeting: An intruder? Excellent. I happened to need fresh organs, and you walked right in.
hostile.farewell: Run all you want, you'll end up on the infirmary floor eventually. You've already been 'classified.'

deathLine: My body... is getting cold... This sensation... must be recorded...
description: A madman of the terminal who wields a scalpel to satisfy destructive curiosity rather than to heal. Rumored to be former Resistance.

heal.title: 💉 [Infirmary Log: Dr. Zed]
heal.success: ✨ HP/MP and all minions have been perfectly restored.
logs: "Can't have the materials spoiling. I've stitched everything back together for now."
"Don't get too happy about it being free. You'll find out what I've planted in your body soon enough."
"Stinks of corpses in here. I've drenched you in disinfectant, so you won't rot for a while."
"I wouldn't have minded if you left a limb or two behind next time. You got lucky."

intro: Dr. Zed: "Wander the corners of the Terminal long enough... and you'll eventually run into those rat-like swarms."
Dr. Zed: "Those who call themselves the 'Resistance.' I once shared a boat with them, long ago..."
already_know: Dr. Zed: "Oh, it seems you've already met those old-fashioned fools."
Dr. Zed: "The cowards who call surgery 'sacrilege' in the name of conviction or human rights."
Dr. Zed: "They fear the possibility of evolution. They can't understand a... 'practical' doctor like me."
Dr. Zed: "Don't get too deep with them. If they saw your body—neither dead nor alive—they'd try to 'save' you. And that would be your most horrific end."
first_time: Dr. Zed: "They're so foolish. They have the technology to save people, yet they're bound by the shackles of 'ethics' and can't even use their tools properly."
Dr. Zed: "Ultimately, I took my scalpel and came here to the Terminal. At least here, I never run out of materials."
Dr. Zed: "You'd better be careful. They see a Necromancer like you as an 'unnatural monster.'"

upgrade.welcome: Dr. Zed: "A magnificent plan! With the essence of my Soul Engineering, I guarantee this Golem's offensive power will reach a level you've never witnessed before!"
machine_hate: Dr. Zed: "Ugh! What are these fishy-smelling hunks of metal?! Get them out of my sight immediately!"
Dr. Zed: "There's no room for my essence because of this scrap metal trash!"
upgrade.menu_remove: 🔪 [Extraction] Remove one Soul stack (Cost: {{cost}} Soul Fragments)
upgrade.limit_reached: Dr. Zed: (Trembling with a scalpel) "No slots? No, if I 'make' one, it exists!"
upgrade.hard_limit: Dr. Zed: "No more. Any more than this and it won't be a golem, just an exploding meat balloon."
upgrade.no_exp: Dr. Zed: "You don't have enough Soul Fragments!"
upgrade.no_soul: Dr. Zed: "I don't see any of my essence here."
upgrade.no_money_remove: Dr. Zed: "You want me to perform an extraction without paying? Go collect more Soul Fragments."
upgrade.success_limit: 💉 [Madness Limit Break Surgery Complete] Dr. Zed: "Hahaha! I shoved it in and it actually fit! Now it finally feels 'alive'!"
upgrade.success_normal: 💉 [Surgery Complete] Dr. Zed: "Hehehe! Look at that! The soul is screaming and thrashing inside the steel!"
upgrade.remove_done: 🧪 [Extraction Complete] Dr. Zed takes the excised tissue with a satisfied smile.
upgrade.exit_msg: Dr. Zed: "Close the door on your way out. Dust is getting in."

awake.already_active: Zed: "It's already active. Duplicate output is just a waste of resources."
dialogues: Zed: ...So this is the core of the Golem that guarded the 3rd floor basement.
Zed: The core is completely shattered. A normal person would've thrown it in the trash...
Zed: But you're lucky. Someone of my caliber can make it pulse once again.
Zed: Now, hand that core over to me. I'll fix it up even stronger than before.
awake.confirm: Injecting Reaper's Mana into the core. The Golem may wake up incomplete and risk going berserk. Proceed?
awake.denied: Zed: "A wise choice. The screaming of the metal hasn't stopped yet."
success: Zed: "System load complete. As you can see... it's become quite a marvelous killing machine."
Zed: "From now on, this scrap heap will follow your shadow and crush anything that stands in your way."
Zed: "If you wish, there may be a way to further enhance it..."

talk1: Oh… so you’re the new Necromancer everyone’s talking about. Pleasure to meet you.
A moving corpse that reeks of death… medically speaking, I’ve been dying to get you on my dissection table.
Knowing how roughly the Reaper runs his lackeys, it won’t be long before you come limping into my clinic with a missing limb or two.
But don’t you worry. My scalpel skills are second to none, so I’ll stitch you back together before your 'materials' spoil.
Until then, do try not to get your brain or heart completely ripped out out there. Hee-hee-hee!
talk2: …What? Charon, that stubborn old fool, finally got purged?
He was impossible to talk to, but he was the only manager who’d turn a blind eye to my eccentric chemical experiments… with him gone, this clinic feels rather empty.
No, what’s even more tragic is that I lost my final chance to experiment on that unyielding soul of his!

The Reaper is truly merciless—reducing such magnificent biological tissue into ashes without leaving a trace… as his long-time colleague, it’s a waste of resources so heartbreaking it brings tears to my eyes.
I’m not blaming you, of course, but next time you clear out a familiar face, at least salvage the corpse intact. I’d gladly show my respects by resurrecting them into a spectacular killing machine.
talk3_1: Oh, I see you successfully rescued the VIPs those Resistance rats were targeting?
Those reckless fools… they actually thought they could take hostages, hold the Reaper by the throat, and force a negotiation. Their little plan collapsed beautifully right from the start.
The Reaper isn’t the gentle type to sit down at a bargaining table over a few hostages anyway. I don’t know why they had to be so utterly foolish and naive.
To be frank, I did briefly find myself hoping that reckless gamble would somehow work out… Ah, never mind. Just an old doctor talking to himself.
Well, the board is already set, so what can we do? If you’re not hurt, go tend to those high and mighty nobles—their blood pressure must have plummeted from all that screaming.
talk3_2: Oof, looking at your miserable state, you must have been completely torn to shreds by the Reaper’s wrath, huh?
Letting the Resistance snatch those precious VIPs right out from under you… just imagining the fury that absolute being unleashed makes even my scalpel tremble.
It’s hilarious to think of those hypocritical VIPs shaking in terror as hostages, but… I never expected those reckless Resistance fools to actually pull off a stunt this massive.
…The Reaper will never negotiate, so a bloody storm is coming. I only hope the price those foolish friends of mine have to pay won’t be too gruesome.
Ah, look at me, this is no time to be worrying about others… Before you get completely dismantled by the Reaper yourself, sit down over here so I can stitch up those nasty tears.
talk4_1: …So, you’ve returned. I don’t even need to ask what kind of 'monstrosity' you destroyed deep within that vehicle depot; I can already feel it.

To think that reckless legacy—the one meant to challenge the realm of the gods—was actually completed and breathing… As someone who once caught a glimpse of its blueprints at the very beginning, I feel quite strange.
The madness that I left unfinished and abandoned… I suppose some obsessed technician left behind actually succeeded in awakening it completely.

To think that grand leap forward was turned into nothing but a heap of scrap metal by the Reaper's favorite hound… As a doctor, a bizarre sense of futility washes over me, cutting straight to my heart.
…Ah, forget what I just said. I must have gotten too caught up in the delusions of those rats. The Reaper is summoning you, so off you go. You're tracking dust everywhere.
talk4_2: …Ha, HAHAHAHA! Do you see it?! That magnificent pulsation! In the end, my design, my scalpel… I wasn't wrong!
I thought you were the Reaper's most loyal hound, yet here you are, leading the vanguard of the rebellion—bringing back that grand legacy I left unfinished when I fled this place, fully operational!
To think the price of turning my back on my past and living like the Reaper’s dog would catch up to me… in the form of such a breathtaking, flawless scientific triumph right before my eyes!
Once this rebellion succeeds and the master of the Terminal changes, a traitorous doctor like me who abandoned his comrades will be the first to be executed and laid out on a cold dissection table.
But I don’t care! There is no better sacrificial lamb than the Reaper to test-run my ultimate masterpiece! Now go, take that absolute being's head, and prove my theory to the world!
